title: Introduction to birddog
text: birddog helps detect emergence trace trajectories scientific literature patents. reads datasets OpenAlex Web Science (WoS), builds citation-based networks, identifies groups, summarizes dynamics. stable release CRAN. development version available GitHub: https://github.com/roneyfraga/birddog.

title: Introduction to birddog
code: # stable version (CRAN) install.packages("birddog")  # development version (GitHub) # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("roneyfraga/birddog") library(birddog)

title: Introduction to birddog
text: birddog supports two main data sources: OpenAlex: browser search CSV export, API via openalexR. Web Science: multiple export formats (.bib, .ris, plain-text .txt, tab-delimited .txt).

title: Introduction to birddog
code: library(openalexR)  # Fetch works from OpenAlex API url_api <- "https://api.openalex.org/works?page=1&filter=primary_location.source.id:s121026525"  openalexR::oa_request(query_url = url_api) |>   openalexR::oa2df(entity = "works") |>   birddog::read_openalex(format = "api") ->   M  # Or from a CSV export M <- birddog::read_openalex("path/to/openalex-export.csv", format = "csv")

title: Introduction to birddog
code: # BibTeX M <- birddog::read_wos("path/to/savedrecs.bib", format = "bib")  # RIS M <- birddog::read_wos("path/to/savedrecs.ris", format = "ris")  # Plain text M <- birddog::read_wos("path/to/savedrecs.txt", format = "txt-plain-text")  # Tab-delimited M <- birddog::read_wos("path/to/savedrecs.txt", format = "txt-tab-delimited")

title: Introduction to birddog
text: use biogas dataset OpenAlex 57,734 documents running example.

title: Introduction to birddog
text: Identify connected components eliminate disconnected documents share bibliographic references.
code: comps <- birddog::sniff_components(net) comps$components |>   dplyr::slice_head(n = 5) |>   gt::gt()

title: Introduction to birddog
text: Detect research communities within citation network. group represents cluster related publications.
code: groups <- birddog::sniff_groups(   comps,   algorithm = "fast_greedy",   min_group_size = 30,   seed = 888L ) groups$aggregate |>   gt::gt()

title: Introduction to birddog
text: Summarize group-level statistics including publication trends growth rates.
code: # ~2 min groups_attributes <- birddog::sniff_groups_attributes(   groups,   growth_rate_period = 2010:2024,   show_results = FALSE ) groups_attributes$attributes_table

title: Introduction to birddog
text: Explore frequent keywords group.
code: groups_keywords <- birddog::sniff_groups_keywords(groups)  groups_keywords |>   dplyr::filter(group %in% c('c1g1', 'c1g2', 'c1g3')) |>   gt::gt()

title: Introduction to birddog
text: Extract key phrases abstracts using natural language processing.
code: # ~30 min groups_terms <- birddog::sniff_groups_terms(groups, algorithm = "phrase") groups_terms$terms_table |>   dplyr::slice_head(n = 3) |>   gt::gt()

title: Introduction to birddog
text: Classify documents role network using Zi-Pi method. Hub documents connect different research communities.
code: # ~20 min groups_hubs <- birddog::sniff_groups_hubs(groups) groups_hubs |> dplyr::filter(zone != "noHub") |> dplyr::mutate(Zi = round(Zi, 2), Pi = round(Pi, 2)) |> dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(zone), dplyr::desc(Zi)) |> dplyr::slice_head(n = 15) |> gt::gt() |> gt::text_transform( locations = gt::cells_body(columns = name), fn = function(x) { glue::glue('{x}') } )

title: Introduction to birddog
text: Measure pace change group tracking old cited references time.
code: # ~1.5 min groups_cct <- birddog::sniff_citations_cycle_time(   groups,   scope = "groups",   start_year = 2000,   end_year = 2024 )  groups_cct$plots[["c1g3"]]

title: Introduction to birddog
text: Track keyword diversity within group time. Increasing entropy signals thematic diversification; decreasing entropy signals convergence.
code: groups_entropy <- birddog::sniff_entropy(   groups,   scope = "groups",   start_year = 2000,   end_year = 2024 )  groups_entropy$plots[["c1g3"]]

title: Introduction to birddog
text: Track research communities evolve time building cumulative networks year following groups consecutive periods.
code: # ~2 min groups_cumulative <- birddog::sniff_groups_cumulative(groups) suppressMessages({   groups_cumulative_trajectories <- birddog::sniff_groups_trajectories(groups_cumulative) })  birddog::plot_group_trajectories_2d(   groups_cumulative_trajectories,   group = "c1g3",   label_vertical_position = -2 )  birddog::plot_group_trajectories_3d(   groups_cumulative_trajectories,   group = "c1g3" )

title: Introduction to birddog
text: Automatically detect highest-scoring temporal paths within group using dynamic programming, display variable-width lines grow cumulative paper counts.
code: traj_data <- birddog::detect_main_trajectories(   groups_cumulative_trajectories,   group = "c1g3" )  traj_filtered <- birddog::filter_trajectories(   traj_data$trajectories,   top_n = 3 )  birddog::plot_group_trajectories_lines_2d(   traj_data = traj_data,   traj_filtered = traj_filtered,   title = "c1g3" )  birddog::plot_group_trajectories_lines_3d(   traj_data = traj_data,   traj_filtered = traj_filtered,   group_id = "c1g3" )

title: Introduction to birddog
text: Identify key route citation network, revealing influential chain documents time.
code: groups_key_route <- birddog::sniff_key_route(groups, scope = "groups") groups_key_route[["c1g3"]]$plot groups_key_route[["c1g3"]]$data |> dplyr::select(-name) |> gt::gt() key_route_c1g3_data |> dplyr::select(document = name, name2, title = TI) |> gt::gt() |> gt::text_transform( locations = gt::cells_body(columns = document), fn = function(x) { glue::glue('{x}') } )

title: Introduction to birddog
text: Detect topics within group using Structural Topic Modeling, creating sub-groups based linguistic similarities. 17 topics best fit.
code: # Prepare STM data (~30 min) groups_stm_prepare <- birddog::sniff_groups_stm_prepare( groups, group_to_stm = "c1g3" ) groups_stm_prepare$plots[['metrics_by_k']] groups_stm_prepare$plots[['exclusivity_vs_coherence']] # Run STM (~35 sec) groups_stm_run <- birddog::sniff_groups_stm_run( groups_stm_prepare, k_topics = 17, n_top_documents = 20 ) groups_stm_run$topic_proportion |> dplyr::mutate(topic_proportion = round(topic_proportion, 3)) |> gt::gt() groups_stm_run$top_documents |> dplyr::group_by(topic) |> dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(gamma)) |> dplyr::slice_head(n = 3) |> dplyr::select(-DI) |> gt::gt() |> gt::text_transform( locations = gt::cells_body(columns = document), fn = function(x) { glue::glue('{x}') } )

title: Detect main temporal trajectories in group-year DAG — detect_main_trajectories
code: detect_main_trajectories(   groups_cumulative_trajectories,   group,   jaccard_min = 0.05,   intra_min = 0.1,   k_out = 2,   alpha = 1,   beta = 0.1,   top_M = 5,   min_len = 3,   use_docs_per_group = TRUE )

title: Detect main temporal trajectories in group-year DAG — detect_main_trajectories
text: groups_cumulative_trajectories List containing three components: groups_similarity: Nested list similarity data group, containing edges , , weight (Jaccard), documents groups_attributes: Nested list node attributes group, containing quantity_papers, prop_tracked_intra_group, tracked_documents, PY.sd, network_until docs_per_group: Data frame mapping group IDs document IDs accurate unique document counting group Character ID group analyze (e.g., "component1_g01") jaccard_min Minimum Jaccard similarity edges (default: 0.05). Higher values create sparser graphs stronger connections. intra_min Minimum proportion tracked documents within group nodes (default: 0.10). Higher values filter weaker nodes. k_out Maximum number outgoing edges keep per node (default: 2). Controls graph sparsity - lower values create simpler backbone structures. alpha Weight edge strength path scoring (default: 1). Higher values emphasize transition strength node quality. beta Per-step persistence bonus path scoring (default: 0.1). Higher values encourage longer trajectories. top_M Maximum number disjoint trajectories extract (default: 5) min_len Minimum number distinct years valid trajectory (default: 3) use_docs_per_group Whether use document IDs accurate unique document counting (default: TRUE). FALSE, uses approximation.

title: Detect main temporal trajectories in group-year DAG — detect_main_trajectories
text: list two components: graph: igraph object representing temporal DAG scoring attributes optional document IDs trajectories: tibble detected trajectories sorted score, columns: traj_id: Trajectory identifier ("tr1", "tr2", ...) start, end: First last year trajectory length: Number distinct years trajectory nodes: List node names along path (e.g., "y2009g03") score: Total path score dynamic programming mean_w: Mean edge score along path sum_docs: Count unique documents covered path mean_size: Mean node size (quantity_papers × proportion tracked) mean_PYsd: Mean publication year standard deviation

title: Detect main temporal trajectories in group-year DAG — detect_main_trajectories
text: function implements comprehensive pipeline detecting significant temporal trajectories research group evolution:

title: Detect main temporal trajectories in group-year DAG — detect_main_trajectories
text: Build Temporal DAG: Constructs directed acyclic graph : Nodes represent group-year combinations filtered intra_min quality threshold Edges represent transitions consecutive years filtered jaccard_min Graph sparsified top k_out edges per node Score Components: Computes node edge scores: Node score: \(s_v = \log(1 + \text{quantity\_papers}_v \times \text{prop\_tracked\_intra\_group}_v)\) Edge score: \(s_e = \text{weight}_e \times \log(1 + \text{documents}_e)\) Extract Trajectories: Uses dynamic programming find heaviest paths: Path score: \(\text{best}(v) = \max\left( s_v, \max_{u \v} \left( \text{best}(u) + s_v + \alpha \cdot s_{(u,v)} + \beta \right) \right)\) Iteratively extracts top top_M disjoint trajectories Trajectories must span least min_len distinct years Count Documents: Calculates unique document coverage: use_docs_per_group = TRUE: Exact count via set union document IDs Otherwise: Approximation: \(\sum \text{node documents} - \sum \text{edge documents}\)

title: Detect main temporal trajectories in group-year DAG — detect_main_trajectories
text: smoother, longer trajectories: Increase beta (persistence bonus) transition-focused scoring: Increase alpha (edge weight) denser connectivity: Lower jaccard_min increase k_out higher quality nodes: Increase intra_min exact document counts: Ensure use_docs_per_group = TRUE provide docs_per_group data

title: Filter and rank detected trajectories — filter_trajectories
text: tr_tbl tibble trajectories detect_main_trajectories()$trajectories. Must contain least traj_id, score, length columns. top_n Maximum number trajectories keep filtering sorting (default: 3). NULL, keeps trajectories meet filter criteria. min_score Minimum score threshold trajectories (default: NULL). Trajectories score less min_score discarded. Useful removing weak noisy paths. min_length Minimum trajectory length distinct years (default: NULL). Trajectories shorter min_length discarded. Ensures trajectories spanning meaningful temporal horizon kept.

title: Filter and rank detected trajectories — filter_trajectories
text: filtered sorted trajectory tibble structure input, containing trajectories meet criteria, sorted descending score. Returns empty tibble trajectories meet criteria.

title: Filter and rank detected trajectories — filter_trajectories
text: function provides straightforward way refine trajectory detection results applying quality filters ranking. filtering process occurs three steps: Quality Filtering: Remove trajectories meet minimum quality standards min_score: Filters dynamic programming path score (higher = better) min_length: Filters temporal span distinct years Ranking: Sort remaining trajectories descending score prioritize significant paths Selection: Keep top top_n trajectories filtering sorting

title: Filter and rank detected trajectories — filter_trajectories
text: Focus strongest signals: Use min_score remove low-confidence trajectories Ensure temporal significance: Use min_length require multi-year evolution Limit visualization complexity: Use top_n focus important paths Progressive refinement: Chain multiple calls different criteria

title: Filter and rank detected trajectories — filter_trajectories
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Get trajectories first traj_data <- detect_main_trajectories(   groups_cumulative_trajectories = my_data,   group = "component1_g01" )  # Basic: Keep top 3 trajectories by score top_trajectories <- filter_trajectories(traj_data$trajectories)  # Keep top 5 trajectories with minimum quality standards quality_trajectories <- filter_trajectories(   tr_tbl = traj_data$trajectories,   top_n = 5,   min_score = 10,   min_length = 4 )  # Keep all trajectories meeting minimum length (no top_n limit) long_trajectories <- filter_trajectories(   tr_tbl = traj_data$trajectories,   top_n = NULL,   min_length = 5 )  # Very strict filtering for high-quality, long trajectories strict_trajectories <- filter_trajectories(   tr_tbl = traj_data$trajectories,   top_n = 3,   min_score = 15,   min_length = 6 )  # Use filtered trajectories for visualization plot_group_trajectories_lines_2d(   traj_data = traj_data,   traj_filtered = quality_trajectories ) } # }

text: traj_data List containing trajectory data generated detect_main_trajectories() components: graph: igraph object containing nodes edges across years trajectories: tibble candidate trajectories (traj_id + nodes list) traj_filtered Filtered trajectories tibble filter_trajectories() containing subset emphasize. Must contain columns: traj_id: trajectory identifiers nodes: list character vectors (ordered time orderable) width_range_hi Width range highlighted trajectory segments (default: c(4, 12)). Segment widths scale cumulative paper counts. width_range_lo Baseline width range used compute constant lowlight width (default: c(1.2, 3)). mean range determines lowlight width. use_raw_papers Whether use raw paper counts z-axis scaling (default: TRUE). Uses log1p() (.e., log(1 + x)) handle zero values. Useful trajectories different growth rates, compressing z-axis better visualize relative trajectory shapes. connect_only_existing_edges Whether draw edges exist graph (default: TRUE). FALSE, draws consecutive node pairs trajectories regardless graph edges. show_labels Whether add end--trajectory labels inside 3D plot (default: TRUE) show_only_highlighted Whether show highlighted trajectories (default: FALSE). TRUE, hides background network lowlight trajectories. label_size Font size trajectory end labels (default: 18) hover_font_size Font size hover tooltips (default: 12) lowlight_width Line width lowlight trajectories background network (default: 1) lowlight_alpha Transparency lowlight elements (default: 0.9) lowlight_color Color lowlight elements (default: "#9AA5B1" - neutral gray) width_by_traj_size Whether scale highlighted trajectory's line width proportionally total paper count (default: TRUE). TRUE, trajectory papers uses full width_range_hi, smaller trajectories proportionally thinner (minimum 25% full range). group_id Optional group identifier display plot title (default: NULL). provided, title becomes "3D Trajectories - group_id".

title: Read and Process OpenAlex data — read_openalex
text: tibble standardized bibliographic columns. Typical output includes: id_short, AU, DI, CR, , DT, DE, AB, C1, TC, SC, SR, PY, DB (source flag: "openalex_csv" "openalex_api"). See Details.

title: Read and Process OpenAlex data — read_openalex
text: CSV mode (format = "csv"): file URL, downloaded temporary file parsing (progress message printed). Selected fields mapped standardized tags: id_short (short OpenAlex ID), SR (= id_short), PY (= publication_year), TI (= title), DI (= doi), DT (= type), DE (= keywords.display_name), AB (= abstract), AU (= authorships.author.display_name), (= locations.source.display_name), C1 (= authorships.countries), TC (= cited_by_count), SC (= primary_topic.field.display_name), CR (= referenced_works, https://openalex.org/ prefix stripped), DB = "openalex_csv". PY coerced numeric; helper column DI2 (uppercase, punctuation-stripped variant DI) added; columns -caps tags placed first DI2 relocated DI. API mode (format = "api"): file must data frame containing least column id; typically returned openalexR::oa_request() + openalexR::oa2df() similar. Records filtered type %% c("article","review") deduplicated id. function derives: id_short (= id without https://openalex.org/ prefix) SR (= id_short); CR: concatenated short IDs referenced_works (semicolon-separated); DE: concatenated keyword names (lower case) keywords; AU: concatenated author names (upper case) authorships; plus core fields PY (= publication_year), TC (= cited_by_count), TI (= title), AB (= abstract), DI (= doi), DB = "openalex_api". result keeps one row per id may include original columns input (via right join), constructing standardized fields .

title: Read and Process OpenAlex data — read_openalex
text: format = "csv" — local path HTTP(S) URL OpenAlex CSV export. format = "api" — data frame produced {openalexR} works entity (usual OpenAlex columns, including list-columns keywords, authorships, referenced_works).

text: network network object analyze. scope = "groups", output sniff_groups(). scope = "network", tbl_graph igraph object sniff_network(). scope Character specifying analysis scope: "groups" multiple groups "network" entire network (default: "groups"). start_year Starting year entropy calculation. NULL, uses minimum publication year found network data. end_year Ending year entropy calculation. NULL, uses maximum publication year found network data. mode Character specifying temporal mode entropy calculation: "annual" Uses publications specific year (default). "cumulative" Uses publications start year. "rolling" Uses sliding window window_size years ending year. window_size Integer specifying rolling window size years (default: 5). used mode = "rolling".

text: list three components: data tibble containing entropy values group year plots list plotly objects visualizing entropy trends group years_range vector start_year end_year used calculations

title: Calculate Entropy Based on Keywords Over Time — sniff_entropy
text: function calculates normalized Shannon entropy (Pielou's evenness index) based Shannon's information theory (Shannon, 1948). temporal scope keyword data depends mode parameter: annual: entropy keywords published specific year. Values tend high (near 1) since within-year distributions typically even. cumulative: entropy keywords published year. Shows long-term trends thematic concentration keyword pool grows. rolling: entropy sliding window recent years. Balances sensitivity recent shifts enough data stable estimates. normalized entropy (Pielou's J') calculated : $$J' = \frac{H}{H_{max}} = \frac{-\sum_{=1}^{n} p_i \log_2 p_i}{\log_2 n}$$ \(p_i\) relative frequency keyword \(\), \(n\) number unique keywords, \(H_{max} = \log_2 n\) maximum possible entropy \(n\) categories. Entropy values range 0 1, : 0 indicates minimal diversity (one dominant keyword) 1 indicates maximal diversity (keywords equally frequent) sudden increase entropy may signal emergence new research topics, decrease suggests thematic convergence.

title: Calculate Entropy Based on Keywords Over Time — sniff_entropy
text: Shannon, C. E. (1948). mathematical theory communication. Bell System Technical Journal, 27(3), 379-423. doi:10.1002/j.1538-7305.1948.tb01338.x Pielou, E. C. (1966). measurement diversity different types biological collections. Journal Theoretical Biology, 13, 131-144.

title: Identify Key Routes in Citation Networks — sniff_key_route
text: network network object class tbl_graph igraph containing citation data, list object generated sniff_groups() scope = "groups" scope Character string specifying analysis scope. Must either "network" (full network analysis) "groups" (group-wise analysis grouped network) n_routes Positive integer specifying number key-route starting edges use (default: 1). iteration selects next-highest SPC edge new starting point extends full path. Higher values reveal auxiliary knowledge diffusion paths divergence-convergence structures (see Liu & Lu, 2012, Figure 8). compact_gaps Logical. TRUE, compresses vertical axis removing empty year gaps publications. produces shorter plots suitable academic publications. Default FALSE (chronological spacing). direction Character. Plot direction: "vertical" (default, top--bottom) "horizontal" (left--right, oldest left).

title: Identify Key Routes in Citation Networks — sniff_key_route
text: list containing group: plot - ggplot2 object visualizing key citation route(s) data - tibble publication details (name, TI, AU, PY) nodes key route(s)

title: Identify Key Routes in Citation Networks — sniff_key_route
text: function implements key-route search Liu & Lu (2012): Computes Search Path Count (SPC) citation link using efficient O(V+E) algorithm based topological sort. SPC measures many source--sink paths traverse link. Selects key-route: link highest SPC value. Searches forward end node key-route, greedily following outgoing link highest SPC, sink reached. Searches backward start node key-route, greedily following incoming link highest SPC, source reached. n_routes > 1, procedure repeated: iteration selects edge next-highest SPC new starting point extends forward backward. Routes can share nodes edges, producing divergence-convergence-divergence structure reveals knowledge flows multiple sub-streams. SPC computed forward[u] * backward[v] edge (u, v), forward[u] counts paths source u backward[v] counts paths v sink (Batagelj, 2003). guarantees significant link always included key-route path.

title: Identify Key Routes in Citation Networks — sniff_key_route
text: Liu JS, Lu LYY. integrated approach main path analysis: Development Hirsch index example. Journal American Society Information Science Technology. 2012;63(3):528-542. doi:10.1002/asi.21692 Batagelj V. Efficient algorithms citation network analysis. University Ljubljana, Institute Mathematics, Physics Mechanics, Department Theoretical Computer Science, Preprint Series. 2003;41:897.
